ddls:
CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the server (e.g., data center, region) */,
  "operating_system" TEXT /* Operating system of the server */,
  "architecture" TEXT /* Hardware architecture of the server */,
  "total_memory" REAL /* Total memory capacity of the server */,
  "storage_capacity" REAL /* Total storage capacity of the server */,
  "last_updated" REAL /* Timestamp of the last update to the server's details */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the server (e.g., active, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "components" (
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each component */,
  "component_name" TEXT /* Name of the component (e.g., CPU, memory, disk) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the component */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the component */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the component */,
  "specifications" TEXT /* Detailed specifications of the component */,
  "last_updated" REAL /* Timestamp of the last update to the component's details */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_data" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance data entry */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server the data belongs to */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component the data belongs to */,
  "start_time" REAL /* Start time of the data collection interval (in seconds) */,
  "end_time" REAL /* End time of the data collection interval (in seconds) */,
  "duration" REAL /* Duration of the data collection interval (in seconds) */,
  "energy_consumption" REAL /* Energy consumption during the data collection interval (in Joules) */,
  "cpu_frequency" REAL /* CPU frequency during the data collection interval (in GHz) */,
  "idle_time" REAL /* Idle time during the data collection interval (in seconds) */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage during the data collection interval (in GB) */,
  "disk_usage" REAL /* Disk usage during the data collection interval (in GB) */,
  "network_bandwidth" REAL /* Network bandwidth usage during the data collection interval (in Mbps) */,
  "load_average" REAL /* Load average during the data collection interval */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the performance data (e.g., normal, warning, critical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "timestamps" (
  "timestamp_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each timestamp entry */,
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance data the timestamp belongs to */,
  "timestamp_start" REAL /* Start timestamp (in seconds) */,
  "timestamp_end" REAL /* End timestamp (in seconds) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("timestamp_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_timestamps_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_id") REFERENCES performance_data ("performance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, viewer) */,
  "created_at" REAL /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" REAL /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the performance data */,
  "access_time" REAL /* Timestamp of the access event (in seconds) */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_id") REFERENCES performance_data ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
